name: File Access
description: Events where a file is opened or accessed, making its contents available to the requester. This includes reading, executing, or interacting with files by authorized or unauthorized entities. Examples include logging file access events (e.g., Windows Event ID 4663), monitoring file reads, and detecting unusual file access patterns.

access_scenarios.description: A user opens a sensitive document on a shared drive.
A script or executable file is accessed and executed.
An unauthorized user attempts to access a protected configuration file.
Bulk access to multiple files in a short time.
access_scenarios.example_context: Mass access to documents on a file server

access_scenarios.description: Files on a network share are accessed remotely.

event_logs.description: Captures file system auditing details, including who accessed the file, access type, and file name.

sysmon.description: Process creation; can provide insight into files executed.

unified_logs.description: Monitor file access using the macOS Unified Logging System.

network_devices.description: Monitor file access over network shares.

siem_integration.action: Collect file access logs from all platforms and centralize in a SIEM for correlation and analysis.

Read access to known backup software configuration files (e.g., /etc/rsnapshot.conf,
                /opt/veeam/config.ini)
